rationale: Structural recursion defines a function F on WFF (def:well-formed-formula) by clauses on each formation case. Its well-definedness/EXISTENCE requires that every formula decompose uniquely into the case used -- i.e. unique readability (thm:unique-decomposition), which was missing. Structural induction (already present) gives the UNIQUENESS of F; unique-decomposition gives the existence/consistency. Added thm:unique-decomposition.
notes: Completes the intended layering: recursive definitions (Var/Sub/conn/depth/extended-truth-assignment) -> thm:structural-recursion -> thm:unique-decomposition -> constructor disjointness+injectivity. No cycle (unique-decomposition and its lemmas do not depend on structural-recursion). This also retroactively justifies why the recursive-definition nodes should anchor on structural-recursion (see 0110/0111 changes): structural-recursion now properly carries the unique-readability they need.
